ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tart class (FOODON_03301710). No specific entry exists for pandan kaya tart; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKR covers pastries and cakes as the nearest defensible parent category for this filled tart product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to pandan kaya tart or a sufficiently close parent pastry concept suitable for clinical coding.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Pandan Kaya Garden Tart piece weighing approximately 80 g, as typically sold individually at Singaporean patisseries and dessert cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Egg tart / custard tart, per 100 g — adapted with pandan kaya composition adjustment using USDA FoodData Central reference 'Egg custard tart' (SR Legacy) and HPB Singapore Foodo kaya-based dessert analogues
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central, SR Legacy) cross-referenced with Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Pandan Kaya Garden Tart exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining: (1) USDA SR Legacy 'Egg custard tart' as the pastry-shell and custard base reference, (2) HPB Foodo kaya toast and kaya-based dessert entries for the kaya cream and coconut-egg jam component, and (3) standard pandan mousse composition derived from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and gelatin. The composite estimate reflects a layered tart with approximately 40% pastry shell, 30% pandan mousse, 20% kaya cream, and 10% lemongrass jelly by weight. Energy and macros are consistent with similar Southeast Asian custard-filled tarts.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pastry and custard tart analogues — estimated from 'Tart shell, shortcrust pastry' and 'Egg custard' GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pandan Kaya Garden Tart. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) shortcrust pastry tart shell GI ~57 (University of Sydney GI Database), (2) egg custard GI ~35–43 (Atkinson et al. 2008), and (3) kaya jam (coconut-egg-sugar spread) estimated GI ~55–60 due to sucrose and coconut milk content. Weighted composite GI of approximately 55 is defensible for this layered dessert. Glycemic load per serving = (55 × 28.4 g available carbohydrate per 80 g serving) / 100 = 15.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~76 mg per 80 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single Pandan Kaya Garden Tart contributes approximately 76 mg sodium per serving, which is low relative to the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals, making occasional consumption acceptable within a sodium-controlled diet.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~14.4 g per serving) and low glycemic index (~55) with significant glycemic load (~15.6)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of this tart due to its high refined sugar content from kaya jam and the low GI of ~55, which produces a glycemic load of approximately 15.6 per serving, potentially causing meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation. While the GI is classified as low, the overall carbohydrate and sugar content contribute to a moderate glycemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~6.2 g per serving) from coconut milk, butter, and egg yolk in kaya
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The Pandan Kaya Garden Tart contains approximately 6.2 g of saturated fat per serving primarily from coconut milk and butter used in kaya and mousse, which represents about 31% of the 20 g daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with CVD should consume this dessert only occasionally.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pandan Kaya Garden Tart is a premium Singaporean artisanal dessert tart featuring multiple distinct layers: a crisp, buttery shortcrust pastry shell, a layer of lemongrass-infused jelly, a silky pandan-flavoured mousse, and a rich pandan kaya cream topping. Kaya is a traditional Singaporean coconut-egg jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaves, and is a beloved local condiment and dessert ingredient. Pandan (Pandanus amaryllifolius) imparts a distinctive fragrant, vanilla-like green flavour that is central to many Southeast Asian sweets. The lemongrass jelly adds a refreshing citrusy herbal note that balances the richness of the mousse and kaya cream. This tart is sold as a single-serve premium item, typically at artisanal patisseries and upscale dessert cafes in Singapore such as Kazo Premium. Nutritionally, one tart (approximately 80 g) provides around 228 kcal, 3.8 g protein, 11.6 g fat, and 28.4 g carbohydrate, with a low glycemic index of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of 15.6. The high sugar content from kaya and the saturated fat from coconut milk and butter make this a treat-category food that should be consumed in moderation, particularly by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The tart is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore's food service context.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Pandan Kaya Garden Tart exists in HPB Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy egg custard tart data and HPB Foodo kaya-based dessert analogues. GI is estimated from analogous pastry and custard tart values in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008). Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is defensible and internally consistent, but direct measurement data for this specific product are not available.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates; no direct laboratory analysis or approved-source exact match exists for this specific product. Values should be treated as indicative and updated if HPB Foodo or a peer-reviewed source publishes data for pandan kaya tart specifically. 2. Serving size of 80 g is estimated based on typical single-serve artisanal tart weights in Singapore; the actual weight of the Kazo Premium product may differ. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy egg custard tart reference or the HPB Foodo kaya dessert analogues used for this estimate. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources used for this composite estimate. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary reference sources used for this composite estimate. 6. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this product has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature as of the accessed date. 7. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singaporean artisanal patisserie practice; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 8. The food_name has been normalised from the menu-style input 'Pandan Kaya Garden Tart – 1pc' to the canonical form 'Pandan Kaya Garden Tart' by removing the portion descriptor.

improvement_actions_taken: Normalised food_name from menu-style 'Pandan Kaya Garden Tart – 1pc' to canonical 'Pandan Kaya Garden Tart'.
Added all mandatory Singaporean local-language names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy and HPB Foodo analogues; documented estimation methodology in nutrient_source.
Assigned GI of 55 (medium) from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) analogous estimate; computed GL = 15.6 per 80 g serving.
Selected defensible FoodOn (FOODON_03301710) and FoodEx2 (A0CKR) codes as closest_parent; SNOMED CT set to null with gap note.
Populated all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance; Type 2 Diabetes flagged as high concern.
Wrote natural_language_description exceeding 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
Built comprehensive aliases list and aliases_by_language object; verified cross-consistency between flat list and structured object.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-absence justification in notes.
Set ontology match_type values exclusively to allowed values: closest_parent and none.

Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification (55 or less is low GI). Updated related text in gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes (key_factor and guidance), and llm_training.natural_language_description to reflect this change.
